name: mostParamIsAvailable
title: mostParamIsAvailable
syntax: long mostParamIsAvailable(mostAmsMessage * msg, char paramadr[]); // form 1
long mostParamIsAvailable(mostAmsMessage * msg, char paramadr[], long arrayIndex); // form 2
description: Check whether a described parameter with the parameter name from the XML function catalog is in an AMS message.
PosDescription, TelLen and StreamCases are checked in addition to FblockID, FunktionID and OpType.
This function can be used to prevent erroneous access to messages that can be first identified at run time of a CAPL program.

parameters.name: msg
parameters.description: Message to which parameter access should be applied.

parameters.name: paramAdr
parameters.description: Identification of parameters (see Symbolic Identification of Parameters).

parameters.name: arrayIndex
parameters.description: Parameter index in arrays or sequences. This declaration overwrites the indexing in the brackets of <paramAdr>.

description: In the following sample, access to a parameter is assured in a received message. If the status message is partially transmitted, either through targeted use of the Pos parameter or erroneous assignment of TelLen, it can happen that StartTrack is not part of the received message.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
